title: GitHub Integration: Publish SQL to Repository
story: As a data migration specialist, I want to publish generated SQL from the migration tab directly to a GitHub repository so that my SQL transformations are version controlled and available for CI/CD pipelines.
description:
Integrate DataTools Pro with GitHub to allow users to publish generated SQL code directly from the migration tab's SQL generation section to a GitHub repository.

The integration requires:
- GitHub personal access token (stored encrypted in the database)
- Repository owner name
- Repository name

Publishing behavior:
- First publish: creates a new file in the repository named {sourceobject}_{salesforceobject}.sql
- Subsequent publishes: commits changes to the existing file (only if the SQL content has changed)

This is an end-to-end MVP connecting DataTools Pro's SQL generation capability with GitHub's version control. Future extensions could include Snowflake-to-Salesforce data mapping version control.

Reference: https://datatoolspro.com/tutorials/snowflake-to-github/
acceptance_criteria: User can enter a GitHub personal access token, repository owner, and repository name
Token is stored encrypted in the database
A 'Publish Code to GitHub' button appears in the SQL generation section
First publish creates a new file named {sourceobject}_{salesforceobject}.sql
Subsequent publishes commit only changed content to the existing file
Connection is validated before saving credentials
User receives confirmation after successful publish
ux_changes: New 'Publish Code to GitHub' button in the migration tab's SQL generation section. Settings form for entering GitHub token, repository owner, and repository name. Publish confirmation feedback.
middleware_changes: GitHub API client for repository operations: file creation (first publish) and content update (subsequent publishes). Diff detection to only commit when SQL content has actually changed. Token encryption for secure storage.
data_model_changes: New table to store encrypted GitHub credentials (personal access token, repository owner, repository name) per team/account.
api_changes: Outbound calls to GitHub API: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path} for checking existing files, PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path} for creating/updating files.
implementation_notes: File naming convention is {sourceobject}_{salesforceobject}.sql for version-controlled SQL transformations. The integration checks if the file already exists and uses the SHA of the existing file for updates (GitHub API requirement). Token encryption is mandatory for storage.
comment_summary: Developer built the GitHub publish functionality with file creation and update capabilities. Product owner confirmed the use case is publishing SQL from the migration tab's SQL generation feature. File naming was refined to sourceobject_salesforceobject.sql. Feature was ready for review on development.
